title: Heaven or Hell Lets Rock
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 15, 2007 3:19 pm Shamonan wrote: Rei/Shinji is kind of nice, since...

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 5:34 pm I think he tries to strangle her like in the apartment sequence because she isn't glad to see him; that he gave up Heaven to be with her on a dark and forgotten planet and she's still acting catatonic. However, when she responds and caresses Shinji's face, he lets go of her, breaks down and cries, because he knows he's made the wrong decision in deciding for the both of them to retain their corporeal forms. Instead of giving into Instrumentality, living forever in bliss with Rei, Kaworu, and his mother, Shinji chose the pleasures of Asuka's flesh. When she in turn realizes that Shinji hasn't changed, and it's his fault for effectively damning them to become the new Adam and Eve, she says her famous line: "How disgusting". She's too disgusted to even get mad at him. I like The End of Evangelion, but can you see why I like the end of the TV series more? :( To me, the movie is an alternate ending.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Apr 15, 2007 5:49 pm Instrumentality isn't heaven or bliss. Instrumentality is the Collective. Resistance is futile.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 5:52 pm BobBQ wrote: Instrumentality isn't heaven or bliss. Instrumentality is the Collective. Resistance is futile.

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 5:58 pm I guess that's one way of looking at it. I don't think it looks that bad, though. If your body turns into Tang, at least you'll go to Heaven if you lived a good life. It's certainly better than ending up like Shinji and Asuka. I just described how being trapped in the flesh made them weaker.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:03 pm Shamonan wrote: If your body turns into Tang, at least you'll go to Heaven if you lived a good life.

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:18 pm The Heaven that Lilith sent people to in The End of Evangelion. You catch a glimpse of it when Kaworu and Rei are trying to persuade Shinji to follow them. As for how Shinji is 'stronger' for staying on Earth, being selfish isn't true strength at all. Even if Instrumentality were the easy way out, what of it? Even if you don't consider it to be Heaven, it's certainly better than living under a perpetually black sky, devoid of civilization, surrounded by a sea of LCL and crucified Evangelions. Even Shinji realized what an idiot he was, even if it were at the very last moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:23 pm Instrumentality was a world of false happiness. It takes much more strength to live in the world of pain that accept the cop-out that is HIP. We don't even know how badly damaged the planet is, but that's a debate I'd rather not start again.

[]:             Quote Post by Shamonan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 6:26 pm Well, you can't convince me that Instrumentality is false happiness, but I respect your opinion. In fact, I much rather prefer the version of Instrumentality that Shinji manufactures in the end of the TV series, with everyone living normal lives, etc. It's a much more optimistic ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Apr 15, 2007 8:17 pm Shamonan wrote: Well, you can't convince me that Instrumentality is false happiness, but I respect your opinion. In fact, I much rather prefer the version of Instrumentality that Shinji manufactures in the end of the TV series, with everyone living normal lives, etc. It's a much more optimistic ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 15, 2007 8:56 pm What AEF said. And this drives me mad beyond belief, so I must point this out: Shinji & Asuka /=/ ADAM & EVE!!!! Where do people get this crap from?! NOWHERE is it stated in EoE! 26' Will to live on stuff wrote: Rei (OFF): If their hearts can create their own image...they will be able to restore their human bodies . YUI (OFF):It will be alright. All lifeforms have the power to restore themselves...and the wish to live. If you decide to live, anywhere can be heaven...because you're alive. There will be chances to be happy everywhere. As long as the Sun, Moon and Earth exist......it will be alright.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 16, 2007 1:58 am Shamonan wrote: Well, you can't convince me that Instrumentality is false happiness, but I respect your opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 16, 2007 4:12 am Mr. Tines wrote: Yes -- EoTV looks like the fragmentation of Instrumentality has yielded a complete and successful Upload/Friendly-SysOp type Singularity event; as opposed to the non-Friendly (crash and burn, privileged single human, Stop-button-enabled) event of EoE. EoTV's closing scene is more reminiscent of the life in the polein in that strand of Greg Egan's works.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 16, 2007 5:28 pm What is this Heaven that you speak of in EoE? I think you're confusing Yui's "If you decide to live, anywhere can be heaven...because you're alive." with people's POV that Instrumentality is Heaven and Reality is Hell. Like I stated before, Shinji wasn't Damned or anything: He CHOOSE to return after realizing Instrumentality wasn't right. (Again, how is being part of the Collective Consciousness a good thing when you don't exist (as yourself)??) The fact that he returned and later Asuka returned implies that ANYBODY WHO WANTS TO can return (Not that Adam & Eve crap that people who aren't paying attention to think up), the show isn't East of Eden or any of that Pointless Religious Symbolism in that sense. All I have to say on the matter.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 16, 2007 5:51 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The fact that he returned and later Asuka returned implies that ANYBODY WHO WANTS TO can return (Not that Adam & Eve crap that people who aren't paying attention to think up), the show isn't East of Eden or any of that Pointless Religious Symbolism in that sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 16, 2007 5:56 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: Of course, there is no guarantee that anyone else will come back. If that's the case, then Shinji and Asuka as the new Adam and Eve would be correct.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 16, 2007 6:02 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: BrikHaus81 wrote: Of course, there is no guarantee that anyone else will come back. If that's the case, then Shinji and Asuka as the new Adam and Eve would be correct.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Mon Apr 16, 2007 6:03 pm I thought this thread was going to be about DMC instead its about what instrumentality is. Didn't we agree eons ago, that instrumentality isn't heaven?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 16, 2007 6:05 pm AEF wrote: How can there be a new Adam and Eve if there never was an Adam and Eve in the first place? I think this is the fact being overlooked here.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 16, 2007 6:14 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I think AEF's talking about the stuff we don't see part in-between Yume no Sukima and I Need You.
